item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: No disputes. I approve. :D

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Two link proposals here. First one is pretty simple. We link the GOC mention to the GOC hub. Second, since Dr. Everwood is a recurring character on the wiki, we can link to a definitive tale/article for the character, such as SCP-4224. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

Dr. Jonas defected from the Global Occult Coalition in 20██, and expressed discontent with the results of the test. Dr. Jonas requested a repeat of the test.

To this:

Dr. Jonas defected from the Global Occult Coalition in 20██, and expressed discontent with the results of the test. Dr. Jonas requested a repeat of the test.

Editing this:

Due to the minimal security measures in Site 55's Low-Risk Item Storage and the low foot traffic in the area, the continued production of SCP-3803-1 instances went unnoticed for several days until Dr. Everwood attempted to enter the Low-Risk Item Storage and was buried under thousands of SCP-3803-1 instances. Dr. Everwood sustained minor injuries and was rescued by responding security personnel. Through the combined efforts of 2 security and 5 D-Class personnel, SCP-3803 was recovered and production of SCP-3803-1 stopped.

To this:

Due to the minimal security measures in Site 55's Low-Risk Item Storage and the low foot traffic in the area, the continued production of SCP-3803-1 instances went unnoticed for several days until Dr. Everwood attempted to enter the Low-Risk Item Storage and was buried under thousands of SCP-3803-1 instances. Dr. Everwood sustained minor injuries and was rescued by responding security personnel. Through the combined efforts of 2 security and 5 D-Class personnel, SCP-3803 was recovered and production of SCP-3803-1 stopped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2,000,000,000 is a very, very big number.

At a rate of 10 cards every 30 seconds, that works out to 28,800 per day. It would take over 190 years to reach two billion.

I love the imagery of the doctor opening the door and being hit with an cartoon-style avalanche of business cards, but the timeline just doesn't work. Maybe a throwaway line in the incident report saying the rate of card manifestation accelerated would do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my new favorites. Shame that Bright wasn't part of the experiment though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Done! All three images now total 287.74 kB.

I have the larger versions saved, so if you needed the backups, just send me a wikidot message.

item.title: Re: Learn To Recycle
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure he was joking. o:

item.title: Re: Learn To Recycle
item.content:encoded: Would they really recycle anomalous items, even if they're just business cards? CAN they even be recycled?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be excellent. Thank you, Zyn. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll see what I can do though I must admit I am underqualified to perform this myself.

I can help with resizing the images. Would you like me to do so?

item.title: Learn To Recycle
item.content:encoded: yo why did the foundation not recycle all those business cards. Downvote cuz the foundation dont know how to recycle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Something about the simplicity of the anomaly is something that isn't usually done. Most of the time, it's just "THIS SCP CAN MURDER A MAN IN 235526 DIFFERENT WAYS!1!!!," I appreciate the variety. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
6mb? Yikes!
I'll see what I can do though I must admit I am underqualified to perform this myself.
This post will be updated once I find a solution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it be possible to reduce the size of some of these images? They're all above 1 MB (with one at 6MB !), which can take awhile to load for people with slower internet or on mobile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an easy upvote; a simple and solid idea taken to some interesting applications and logical conclusions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now let's see Paul Allen's card.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this was actually the original containment procedure until I pointed out to them that rubber bands deteriorate and putting it in a tiny box would be more reliable

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very satisfying, especially the last minute revelation that there are mundane world consequences. It's not just a box what does magic stuff, it has depth and character and something of an attitude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to say, I'm not sure why they would rely on a D-Class to properly store an object. You'd think it would be handed to a supervising researcher first.

But the fact that it's not just making shit out of ether is a great way to end the article. :D +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one's simple but I like it a lot. Something about "currently being held shut with a rubber band" is really funny to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Corrected. Thank you so much. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really nice!

But there's a small mistake here (probably an autocorrect goof).

Instances of SCP-3803-1 are made of high-grade cardstock, posses simple designs, and are double-backed.

Should be possess.

But anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple idea, excellent execution. I really enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
really glad i could help! thanks for the super sweet insert, glad to see people are looking this ♥️

likewise, the business card designhas a .psd template - if anyone wants their own, feel free to message me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oopsy daisy, my bad. I'm fixing it now.
Thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good!

I did notice that you have both Calloway and Callaway where I believe you are talking about the same person. I would have quickly fixed it, but I'm not sure which is the intended name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm flattered. Thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this! Simple anomaly but extremely effective execution, especially in the test logs. You got a genuine laugh at “in over her head” from me, earning you an instant upvote. I’m really excited to see what you come up with next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to daisybellus for helping so much with crit and taking the picture for the article themselves. I could not have done this without their attention to detail. The other two photos are from Wikimedia Commons.

This was inspired by my time as a white-collar corporate worker dealing with what seemed to be endless business cards flooding my person at all times.

E: Additional thanks to Zyn for resizing the images to make this article load faster for mobile users.
